mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate lower borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 7.5% signals tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with UNRATE < 7.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust supply-side expansion, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to produce positive SPX 20d returns in > 60% of cases or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate above 4.5% during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) triggers monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as higher rates dampen equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5% in > 50% of cases or the interaction term's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 100 signals stronger manufacturing activity, which drives S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days via improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns fail to show positive correlation with INDPRO increases (correlation < 0.1) or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) prompts central banks to raise rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL > 2.5% in > 50% of cases or the signal's IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,000 thousand units signals strong consumer demand, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days through construction sector growth and wealth effects.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no positive correlation with HOUST spikes (correlation < 0.05) or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and employment (PAYEMS) indicates strong demand-side momentum, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days via sustained economic expansion.
failure_condition: The joint growth of RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce positive SPX 60d returns in > 60% of cases or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).
